learning_outcomes.description: Select appropriate composite material systems to meet the requirements of specific applications.
Determine suitable manufacturing methods for composite materials based on application, cost, and performance considerations.
Apply composite mechanics and design principles to engineer composite structures.
Specify appropriate destructive and non-destructive testing methods for quality assurance during manufacturing and through-life support.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment applies the knowledge and skills developed during the first part of the course (Materials and Manufacturing) to the design of the COMET (Composite Materials Engineering Testbed) unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV).

This is an individual assessment. Each student will be assigned a specific UAV component group (e.g. wing structure, fuselage, control surfaces, propulsion integration or payload structure) and will evaluate suitable engineering materials, manufacturing processes and associated design considerations. Students are expected to justify their recommendations based on material properties, manufacturing capability, structural performance, cost, sustainability and other relevant engineering criteria.

The findings are to be presented in the form of an engineering poster, summarising the design requirements, material selection process, manufacturing evaluation and the recommended engineering solution. The poster should communicate the information in a concise, visually effective and technically rigorous manner suitable for a professional engineering audience.

A detailed introduction to the project will be provided during the studio in Week 2. This will include the marking criteria for the poster. It will also be made available on Blackboard.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Solve problems related to composite material constituents and the elastic behaviour of composite materials using analytical methods and numerical tools.

Please refer to Blackboard for detailed marking criteria.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

assessments.assessment_title: Workshop and NDT Studio participation assessment
assessments.task_description:
This assessment evaluates the learning objectives associated with the workshop and NDT studio activities. The proposed assessment is marked out of 100 and is based on student participation and engagement in the hands-on components. 50 marks are available for the workshop component, and 50 marks are available for the NDT studio activities.

A maximum of 100 marks may be awarded for the quality of participation. The proposed marking scale is as follows:

·        0 marks – Non-attendance or disruptive behaviour.

·        50 marks – Attendance with limited engagement or contribution to the activities.

·        100 marks – Strong engagement, constructive participation, and active contribution throughout the practical activities.

This assessment is intended to encourage active participation while recognising the importance of professional conduct, teamwork, and engagement in practical engineering activities.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessed by teaching staff during the workshop and studio sessions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
